article.body_preview: Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission (BERC) has just hiked the price of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) by 21.59 per cent. This is the highest jump in LPG price in a single go, in the history of LPG price increase in Bangladesh. So, from February's end, consumers will be forking out Tk1,498 per 12kg cylinders, an increase of Tk266 per cylinder. Of course, BERC has its rationale for increase of price, but that is not the point here. At a time when prices of all fuels, starting from octane, petro

llm_connections.evidence_quote: factoring in the fluctuations in foreign-exchange rates and changes in value-added-tax (VAT).
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly states that fluctuations in foreign-exchange rates were factored into the adjusted domestic LPG price, matching the feature's focus on exchange-rate volatility affecting import-related costs.
